findings.title: Windowed audit reads drop still-valid get_rules events after the first call
findings.description: readAuditLogWindowed only filters the newly appended tail once a cursor exists. The function comment says it needs the entire visible window, but the implementation returns only newEntries. A second edit-intent append inside the same 5 minute window can miss an earlier get_rules event and report false non-compliance.
findings.snippet: return newEntries.filter((e) => ts - e.ts <= windowMs && e.ts <= ts);
findings.suggested_issue: Retain or reconstruct the full active time window when a cursor exists, then add a regression test covering two edit-intent appends within one audit window.

findings.title: fab_update_registry rejects valid priority values
findings.description: The tool input schema and TypeScript type define data.priority as number, but the persisted agents meta schema only accepts 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'. Passing the documented enum value fails tool validation, while passing a number survives tool validation but fails in updateRegistry when agentsMetaNodeSchema parses the node.
findings.suggested_issue: Align fab_update_registry inputSchema and UpdateRegistryInput with agentsMetaNodeSchema so add-node and update-node accept the same priority enum end-to-end.

findings.title: MCP event lines in .intent-ledger.jsonl defeat CLI duplicate suppression
findings.description: HTTP mode appends MCP transport events into .intent-ledger.jsonl, while ledger-append de-duplicates only against the last physical line in that file. If the tail line is kind='mcp-event', hasMatchingTailEntry returns false and the same human commit intent can be appended again on the next pre-commit run.
findings.suggested_issue: Either separate MCP transport storage from the human/AI intent ledger or make hasMatchingTailEntry scan backward to the most recent real ledger entry.
